title: Lesson 24 — The Banquet

words.example: A surfeit of choices left no one able to decide.

words.definition: to satisfy fully, or to excess
words.example: The enormous meal satiated even the hungriest guest.

words.definition: an excessively abundant supply of something
words.example: A glut of apples that autumn sent prices crashing.

words.definition: to become sickening or distasteful through excess, especially of something sweet
words.example: The dessert's heavy sweetness soon began to cloy.

words.definition: to eat greedily until completely full
words.example: They gorged on the buffet for the better part of an hour.
A temperate eater, she never once overindulged.

words.definition: sparing or moderate, especially in eating and drinking
words.example: An abstemious host who barely touched the wine.

words.definition: average in amount or degree; not extreme
words.example: A moderate appetite and famously modest tastes.

words.definition: inclined to allow excessive pleasure, comfort, or leniency
words.example: An indulgent grandparent who spoiled the children rotten.

words.definition: a person devoted to pleasure and self-gratification
words.example: A cheerful hedonist forever chasing the next comfort.

words.definition: a person who takes particular pleasure in fine food and drink
words.example: An epicure who could name the vineyard from a single sip.

words.definition: wanting or devouring great quantities; insatiable
words.example: A voracious reader and an even more voracious eater.

story: The banquet offered a [[surfeit|surfeit]] of everything, enough to [[satiate|satiate]] a small army. There was a [[glut|glut]] of rich dishes — so many that the sweetness began to [[cloy|cloy]] by the third course. Some guests [[gorge|gorged]] shamelessly; others stayed [[temperate|temperate]] and [[abstemious|abstemious]], taking only a [[moderate|moderate]] plate. The host, an [[indulgent|indulgent]] [[hedonist|hedonist]] and famous [[epicure|epicure]], watched his most [[voracious|voracious]] friend return for a fifth helping and beamed with pride.
